Fern Por-rrrcAr- PnAcrrcgs CoMMrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramenro. CA g5gll-232g
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
March 26,2008
Mario Juarez
Mario Juarez for
Oakland City Council, District 5
40304 International Blvd.
Oakland, CA 94601
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-08-031
Dear Mr. Juarcz
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Government cocle Secrions 8 I000 through g l0l4. All
statutory ref'erences are kr the Government Code. unless otherw'ise indicated. The regulationi of the Fair
Political Practices commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 rhrough ltt997 of ritle 2 of the Calitirrnia
Cbde of Regulations. AII regtrlakrry ref'erences are to Title 2. Dir ision' 6 of the Calitbrnia Code of
Regulatirtns. unless otherwise indicated.
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "nct"l.' This letter shofld not be construed as assistance
on any conduct that may have already taken place. (See Regulation 1g329(bXgXA).) In
addition' this letter is based on the facts presented. The f'aii political practices
Commission (the "Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when it renders
assistance. (In re Oglesby (I975) I FppC Ops. 71.)
QUESTION
Is a payment to advertise your business that is made by your business an
"expenditure" Llnder the Act and subject to the requirements of the Act?
CONCLUSION
Based upon the facts you have provided. it appears that the payment for the
advertisement is for a personal pllrpose unrelated to your candiclacy for elective office.
Barring additional facts, the payment is not an "expenditure" subjelt to the provisions of
the Act.


File No. .4-08-031
Page No. 2
FACTS
You are a candidate for the Oakland City Council on the June 3, 200g ballot. you
also own a real estate company in Oakland named "The Mario Jnarez Selling Team.,,
Given the current real estate market, you find that marketing has become inc-reasingly
important to your company's survival. tn a telephone conversation on March 13, 200g,
you asked that we consider whether a planned advertisement consisting of a flier and a
tomato plant, delivered to residents wiihin a target area that encompasses potential voters
in the upcoming election, would be subject to th; Act.
ln regards to the advertisement, you stated that you have used similar
advertisements in the past recalling at least two times in the last couple of years.
Additionally, you have used similar advertisements accompanied by a plant in prior
marketing activity in other geographic locations.
The plants themselves are of nominal value and will cost your business less than
one dollar per plant. A flyer will be included with each plant. This flyer will make only
tw-o references to you personally. These references are a short note that the plants are a
gift from The Mario Juarez Selling Team and the address to your business,s website,
"www.mariojuarez.com." The remainder of the flyer will consist of instructions for the
care of the plants.
You have stated that the flyer will contain no references to your candidacy, to the
elected office for which you are running, or to your qualifications for office. In terms of
the target area for the advertisements, you have stated that the target area was determined
based upon the distance from your business, that you have used tf,e same target area for
previous advertisements, and that the target area was not selected because it lncompasses
potential voters in the upcoming election. Furthermore, you have stated that the timing of
the advertisements was selected in consideration of the giowing season for tomatoes and
that the timing of the advertisement is unrelated to that of youriandidacy or upcoming
election.
AI\ALYSIS
The Act defines "expenditure" a1 "a payment, a forgiveness of a loan, a payment
of a loan by a third party, or an enforceable promise to makle a payment, unless it is clear
from the surrounding circumstances that it ii not made for potiticat purposes.,, (Section
82025.) An expenditure is made for a political plrrpose if ii is "for the purpose of
influencing or attempting to influence the action of the voters for or againsi ttre
nomination or election of a candidate." (Regulation 18225(a)(l).) Furthermore, an
expenditure by a candidate is made for a political purpose "unless it is clear from
surrounding circumstances that the payment was madi for personal purposes unrelated to
his or her candidacy or status as an office holder.,' (Regulaiion Bistixzl.l


File No. A-08-031
Page No.3
Payments by a candidate that are "expenditures" under the Act must be disclosed
as _required by Chapter 4 of the Act. (Section 84100 et seq.) Any candidate for elective
office soliciting or receiving contributions or loans must eitablisir one campaign
contribution accoLlnt from which all expenditures must be made. (Section g5201(e).)
Moreover, all contribtltions or loans made to the candidate, to a person on behalf of the
candidate, or to the candidate's controlled committee and all perional funds of the
candidate utilized to promote the election of the candidate must be deposited into the
campaign contribution account prior to expenditure. (Section g5201(;) and (d).)
Under the facts you have provided, it appears from the surrounding circumstances
that the payment for the advertisement is for a personal purposes unrelateJ to your
candidacy. Accordingly, the payment for the advertisemeni is not an'.expenditu.",,under
the Act and falls outside of the confines of the Act.2
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Brian G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division
BGLjgl
t Thit advice is applicable and conf'ers immunity only to the extent that the facts provided to us
are correct' and that all of the material facts have been diiclosed. (lrt re oglesby,.srrpra: Sectign g3 11.1.)
Should tacts exist or emerge that indicate the advertisement is tbr a political pripnr"r. the advice contained
in this letter does not apply.
